changeSet.author: research-edc
changeSet.sql: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION sync_module_rule_to_rule() RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$ BEGIN IF (TG_OP = 'INSERT') THEN INSERT INTO rule (rule_id, oc_oid, name, description, enabled, rule_expression_id, status_id, owner_id, date_created, date_updated, update_id, version) VALUES (NEW.rule_id, NEW.oc_oid, NEW.name, NEW.description, NEW.enabled, NEW.rule_expression_id, NEW.status_id, NEW.owner_id, NEW.date_created, NEW.date_updated, NEW.update_id, NEW.version); RETURN NEW; ELSIF (TG_OP = 'UPDATE') THEN UPDATE rule SET oc_oid=NEW.oc_oid, name=NEW.name, description=NEW.description, enabled=NEW.enabled, rule_expression_id=NEW.rule_expression_id, status_id=NEW.status_id, owner_id=NEW.owner_id, date_created=NEW.date_created, date_updated=NEW.date_updated, update_id=NEW.update_id, version=NEW.version WHERE rule_id=OLD.rule_id; RETURN NEW; ELSIF (TG_OP = 'DELETE') THEN DELETE FROM rule WHERE rule_id=OLD.rule_id; RETURN OLD; END IF; END; $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql; CREATE TRIGGER trg_sync_module_rule AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE OR DELETE ON module_rule FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION sync_module_rule_to_rule();
changeSet.rollback: DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_sync_module_rule ON module_rule; DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS sync_module_rule_to_rule();
